title: The Holocaust
description: Systematic state-sponsored genocide targeting Jews, Roma, disabled people, and others. Death camps including Auschwitz, Treblinka, and Sobibor were built. The Nuremberg trials established international law for war crimes.
outcome: Nazi leaders tried at Nuremberg. Germany pays ongoing reparations.

title: Rwandan Genocide
description: Within 100 days Hutu extremists massacred Tutsi citizens using machetes, guns, and clubs. Radio broadcasts directed killers to specific locations. The UN and international community failed to intervene.
outcome: International Criminal Tribunal for Rwanda prosecuted perpetrators.

title: Cambodian Genocide
victims: Educated classes ethnic minorities and religious groups
description: Pol Pot emptied cities and forced people into labor camps. Intellectuals were executed. Roughly 25% of Cambodia's entire population was killed in four years.
outcome: Khmer Rouge leaders tried decades later. Pol Pot died under house arrest.

title: Armenian Genocide
description: Armenians were deported across the Syrian desert. Death marches mass shootings and starvation killed hundreds of thousands. Turkey still disputes the genocide classification.

title: Bosnian Genocide / Srebrenica
perpetrators: Bosnian Serb forces under Ratko Mladic
description: During the Bosnian War Serb forces under Mladic systematically killed over 8000 Bosniak men and boys in Srebrenica alone. The UN declared it Europe's worst atrocity since WWII.
outcome: Mladic convicted of genocide in 2017 and sentenced to life.

title: Darfur Genocide
description: The Sudanese government armed Arab militias to attack non-Arab communities. Villages burned entire populations displaced and women systematically raped. ICC issued arrest warrant for President Bashir.
outcome: Conflict ongoing. Omar al-Bashir arrested in Sudan in 2019.

title: The Holodomor
description: Stalin deliberately engineered a famine by collectivizing farms and seizing grain. Millions of Ukrainians starved to death. The Soviet Union denied the famine for decades.
outcome: Recognized as genocide by Ukraine and many nations. Russia denies.

title: Guatemalan Genocide
description: Military conducted scorched earth campaigns against Maya villages during civil war. Mass graves found throughout highlands. Rios Montt convicted in 2013 but ruling was overturned.
outcome: Truth commission documented atrocities. Partial justice achieved.

title: Indonesian Mass Killings
description: Following a coup attempt the military and civilian militias killed suspected Communists and their families. Mass graves discovered decades later. CIA provided lists of targets.
outcome: Suharto ruled until 1998. No trials. US government complicity documented.

title: Nanjing Massacre
description: Japanese forces captured Nanjing and engaged in six weeks of mass murder, rape, and looting. Women were systematically raped. The events remain disputed between China and Japan.
outcome: Japan-China tensions persist over historical acknowledgment.
